title: Urban Inequality and Housing Politics
blurb: Cities are sites of profound inequality, where decisions about land, shelter, and infrastructure determine who belongs and who is displaced. My research examines how urban development policies reshape the everyday lives, social relations, and political orientations of communities living at the margins of the city. Drawing on sustained ethnographic fieldwork in Indonesian cities, I focus on how marginalised residents navigate and contest the urban transformations that restructure their worlds, contributing to scholarship on informality, housing justice, displacement, and urban citizenship in the Global South.

title: Youth, Citizenship, and Political Participation
blurb: My research places youth at the centre of political analysis, asking what political life looks like from the standpoint of those most affected by structural inequality yet least recognised as political actors. Rather than measuring youth against idealised models of civic engagement, I examine how young people from disadvantaged communities develop and enact political agency across formal, informal, and everyday registers of participation. Drawing on participatory and youth-centred methodologies that treat young people as knowledge producers, this work contributes to debates on citizenship, voice, and democratic participation in Southeast Asia.

title: Climate Justice and Environmental Governance
blurb: My research examines how marginalised urban youth in Indonesia encounter the disproportionate impacts of climate change and how they make sense of these experiences within their everyday lives. A central concern is the gap between lived climate harm and the political vocabulary needed to name it as injustice.Drawing on epistemic injustice theory, I argue that marginalised communities are systematically denied the platforms and interpretive resources to understand climate harm as structurally produced. This work contributes to scholarship on climate justice and the democratisation of  knowledge, with particular attention to whose voices count in shaping climate policies.
